## Session Summary
Completed doors/windows YOLOv5 model training (50 epochs, 30.3 hours) and tested full PDF-to-Revit pipeline with both wall and door/window detection.

### Key Outcomes
- Doors/windows model training completed - saved to runs/train/doors_windows_v15/weights/best.pt (3.8MB)
- Final metrics: Precision 14.1%, Recall 8.38%, mAP@0.5 4.64% (door: 1.88%, window: 7.4%)
- Updated pdf_to_revit_pipeline.py to use correct doors_windows_v15 model
- Complete pipeline test successful: detecte

When the Revit MCP server (RevitMCPBridge2026) stops responding to requests:
1. The named pipe connects successfully but requests timeout
2. ESC key presses via pyautogui/SendKeys don't trigger the Idling event
3. The ExternalEvent queue gets blocked and won't process new requests
4. Manual restart is required: Go to Revit Add-ins tab → Click Stop MCP → Click Start MCP
5. Alternatively restart Revit entirely
6. Root cause: Revit's Idling event doesn't fire when something is blocking it, even if
recent.summary:

recent.content:
FloorPlanML YOLO mAP issue FIXED:

ROOT CAUSE: Wall bounding boxes were too thin (height or width ~0.01 = 1% of image). YOLO struggles with extremely thin objects.

FIX APPLIED: Modified generate_synthetic_images.py:
1. Added MIN_BBOX_SIZE = 30 pixels (minimum for any dimension)
2. Changed minimum normalized size from 0.001 to 0.03 (3% of image)
3. Applied to walls, doors, windows, and furniture classes

RESULTS:
- BEFORE: mAP_0.5 = 0 (stayed at 0 for all 23 epochs, model learned nothing)
- AFTE

corrections.content:
## Correction Record

### What Claude Said/Did (WRONG):
Wall creation methods (createWall, batchCreateWalls) were failing with "Object reference not set to an instance of an object" error, and I initially thought it was a parameter format issue.

### Why It Was Wrong:
The issue is likely that uiApp.ActiveUIDocument is null - meaning there's no active document in the foreground in Revit, or the user hasn't clicked into the Revit window.

### Correct Approach:
Before attempting wall creation (or any model modification), verify that: (1) Revit has a project open, (2) The project is the active window, (3) No modal dialogs are open. The error happens at line 23: var doc = uiApp.ActiveUIDocument.Document - if ActiveUIDocument is null, accessing .Document throws null reference.

**Category**: code

## Correction Record

### What Claude Said/Did (WRONG):
Started creating a separate Python workflow_orchestrator.py class

### Why It Was Wrong:
Claude Code itself IS the orchestrator. Building a separate Python orchestrator adds unnecessary complexity and doesn't match the architecture. Claude uses knowledge files + memory + MCP methods directly.

### Correct Approach:
Level 3 infrastructure = knowledge files with workflows + memory for patterns + MCP methods. No separate orchestrator code needed. Claude reads knowledge, uses memory, executes methods.

**Category**: architecture

next_steps: - Improve model accuracy with GPU training or larger model (YOLOv5s/m instead of nano)
- Test pipeline on real architectural floor plans (not just CubiCasa dataset)
- Integrate with Revit MCP Bridge for end-to-end floor plan to Revit conversion
- Consider training with more diverse floor plan styles for better generalization
- Restart Revit to load updated DLL
- Test viewport capture methods with a Revit model open
- Test AI rendering integration with Automatic1111 or ComfyUI running locally
- Optional: Add more style presets or custom workflow support
- User should verify shell in Revit 3D view
- Manually add remaining 5 windows if needed
- Investigate transaction/undo issues causing element invalidation
- Consider using batch methods for placing multiple elements
- Add roof to complete basic shell structure
- Test dialog handling fix after Revit restart
- Execute DD→CD workflow on test project
- Measure: time, success rate, retries
- Document results to refine Level 3 patterns
- Test dialog handling fix after Revit restart
- Investigate missing wall types issue
- Build autonomous workflow orchestration layer
- Add error recovery and retry logic to scripts
